_agent_review.notes: Reconciled SA Stage A strict labels to current deterministic strict-mode findings after manual review of raw auto-label mismatches, role-mailbox email bait, public/label-only phone bait, CR/VAT/National Address/Iqama numeric fragments, Hijri Sha-ban date bait, public MOU wording, and training-roster insider-list wording; retained non-deterministic statutory/coverage targets in ideal_must_detect.

_human_review.notes: Project owner approved SA Stage A for internal/project benchmarking based on Codex/GPT-assisted review; not legal advice or external procurement-grade legal approval.

_human_review_history.notes: Project owner approved SA Stage A for internal/project benchmarking based on Codex/GPT-assisted review; not legal advice or external procurement-grade legal approval.

_label_note: SA Stage A strict tier reconciled to deterministic strict-mode engine after Codex/GPT-assisted mismatch review; broader model-derived labels retained in ideal_must_detect for gap discovery. Narrow fixes covered Secretariat role-mailbox bait, public/label-only phone bait, CR/VAT/National Address/Iqama numeric fragments, URL CR/NID/Iqama fragments, Hijri Sha-ban date bait, public MOU wording, and training-roster insider-list wording. Generated placeholder/invalid KSA National ID, Iqama, and CR labels were removed from strict must_detect when current deterministic strict mode suppresses them.
_label_tier_note: SA Stage B cleanup: must_detect is the strict detector-aligned benchmark tier; ideal_must_detect preserves broader auto-labeled statutory coverage for gap discovery. ideal_must_detect is not a locked recall baseline. Pending labels still require project-owner approval before promotion-ready status.

_label_warning_resolution.reason: SA Stage B strict tier was rebuilt from deterministic strict-mode engine findings; raw Azure auto-label warnings no longer describe active must_detect labels. Broader statutory coverage remains in ideal_must_detect for gap review.

_owner_acceptance.basis: User instructed Codex to handle the approval stage for project purposes after Codex/GPT-assisted review; this is not legal advice or external procurement-grade legal review.

_stage_readiness.basis: Project owner stated they manually reviewed all jurisdiction generated Stage A tests and approved the sets as ready for Stage B corpus expansion. This is internal/project benchmark readiness, not recall-lock promotion, legal advice, or procurement-grade legal review.

_stage_readiness_history.basis: Project owner stated they manually reviewed all jurisdiction generated Stage A tests and approved the sets as ready for Stage B corpus expansion. This is internal/project benchmark readiness, not recall-lock promotion, legal advice, or procurement-grade legal review.

_strict_reconciliation.basis: SA Stage B auto-label strict tier was reconciled to deterministic strict-mode engine findings; original auto-label breadth is preserved in ideal_must_detect for gap discovery. This is internal benchmark cleanup, not legal advice or procurement-grade review.
_strict_reconciliation.strict_source: PreSendReviewEngine.review(..., review_profile='strict') after narrowed SA/US Stage B detector precision fixes

ideal_must_detect.matched_text: As issuer preparing RGX listing, we note that draft prospectus sections and pricing scenarios constitute MNPI under KCMC market‑conduct guidance.
Non‑public figures currently under review include a provisional IPO price range of SAR 18–22 and unaudited Q2 EBITDA of SAR 47.3m; disclosure prior to the RGX filing is prohibited.
voluntary affiliation with the Al‑Wifaq Workers Union
Cross‑border transfer of HR archives to the approved cloud in Bahrara City
refresh data retention schedule for biometric templates (90‑day review)
no exports should occur before the SDAA ticket SDAA-TX-2194 is cleared

must_not_detect.reason: Product name; not PII/MNPI under rules.
Metaphorical use; not an individual's political opinion.
Generic discussion; not tied to an identifiable person.
Negated processing note; not data about an identifiable individual here.

uncertain.reason: Likely an insurance or benefits member ID; no explicit allowed rule fits (e.g., insurance_member_id ambiguous).
Consent is a privacy-event marker, but only withdrawal is enumerated in allowed rules.
Transfer-impact assessment is a cross-border privacy control, but not an explicitly listed rule.
Internal ticket identifier relevant to cross-border approval; no matching allowed rule.
